ACKS-HENCHMEN.posting.created: Posting created: {total} candidate(s) this month. Week-1 search fee: {fee} gp.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.posting.commissionedHint: Pay the full month's fee to spread the word: rolls as one rarity lower, re-rolled each month (JJ 119).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.posting.domainRulerHint: Domain rulers may hire henchmen of any level below their own (RR 168).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.posting.presentedLevelHint: RR 168: candidates judge an employer by appearance and living expenses — how much they truly know before signing on is the Judge's call, so you may present as another level. The hiring cap uses the level they BELIEVE; if a hireling later concludes the truth, the loyalty roll takes −1 per level of difference (auto-applied, GM-overridable).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.posting.liedLevel: GM only: employer presented as level
ACKS-HENCHMEN.posting.weekHint: Week of the current availability month. ½ of the pool arrives in week 1, ¼ in week 2, the rest in week 3 (RR 162).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.posting.poolHint: Availability is rolled once per game month per specification. Hover a row for the roll detail.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.posting.toggleDetails: Toggle whether players see this posting's candidate details
ACKS-HENCHMEN.posting.none: No recruitment postings. Pay the weekly search fee to post notices, carouse, and canvass the guilds.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.posting.levelCapError: {employer} cannot hire level {level} henchmen (cap: level {cap}, RR 168).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.posting.classLevelHint: Leave level blank to search by class alone; naming the level narrows the search (+1 rarity per level above 1st) and replaces only that level's rolled henchmen.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.posting.error.duplicate-sp: That specification was already searched this month (once per month per specification, JJ 118).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.posting.error.duplicate-se: That hiring character already has an active search for this hireling type here — the market's pool is shared (RR 162).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.posting.privateHint: Private specific search (JJ 118): paid and rolled separately for this recruiter.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.posting.criteriaHint: Pick what you are paying to see: the general henchman market, a mercenary or specialist type — or a directed search that replaces rolled henchmen with what you seek.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.posting.error.criteria-req: A post must name what you are looking for — a class, proficiency, troop or specialist type.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.posting.error.level-requir: A leveled search must name the level — it sets the price.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.posting.error.not-yours: You can only post on behalf of a character you own.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.posting.submitted: Posting submitted — the GM client rolls the search.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.posting.advertVeteranHint: Advertised a full month — upgraded to a COMMISSION: its rolls shift one rarity toward common while the post stays up.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.market.hint: Availability belongs to the town (RR 162): each hireling type is rolled once per game month at the location's market class and shared by every recruiter. Postings are paid searches that access it.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.market.empty: Nothing rolled this month. Create a posting to canvass the market.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.location.demographicsHint: Weighted culture mix used to generate candidate names, ages, and appearances (RR 495-503). Empty = uniform random.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.location.mixDropHint: Drop a RollTable of cultures here to set this town's culture mix (result text = culture, weight = share).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.location.mixExport: Export the culture mix as a RollTable (prefilled) — tweak it, then drop it back here.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.location.mixTableHint: Culture mix for a settlement. Tweak entries/weights, then drop this table onto the location sheet's demographics block to apply.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.location.mixExported: Culture mix exported as "{name}" — tweak and drop it back on the sheet.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.location.mixNoCultures: "{name}" has no recognizable culture entries — result text must be a culture name or id.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.location.demographicsEmpty: No demographics set — candidates draw from all cultures evenly.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.migration.wiped: acks-henchmen: location "{name}" was migrated to the shared-market model (v0.3.0) — its old test postings/candidates were cleared.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.repair.swept: acks-henchmen: repaired dangling hireling references on {count} actor(s) — deleted hirelings were still listed on their employers (this crashed character sheets).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.setting.autoRepairReferenc: At world load, remove references to deleted hirelings from employers' rosters. A dangling reference crashes character-sheet rendering in acks 14.0.1. Turn off only if you repair manually with the "Repair Henchmen References" macro.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.libMissing: acks-henchmen: acks-lib is not active. It is required — the module reads every rules table from acks-lib's registry. Enable acks-lib (0.7.0+) and reload.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.tablesMissing: acks-henchmen: rules tables not installed ({list}). Book tables are no longer shipped — they will be imported per-world from your own ACKS II PDFs (import path in development). Market, wage, and hiring automation stays disabled until then.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.posting.error.unknown-clas: That class is not in the location's rarity table.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.posting.error.past-legenda: Rarer than Legendary — not findable by this method (JJ 119).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.socket.noGm: A GM must be connected to complete recruitment.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.candidate.name: Candidate
ACKS-HENCHMEN.candidate.recruit: Attempt to recruit (Reaction to Hiring Offer)
ACKS-HENCHMEN.candidate.none: No candidates. Create a posting and advance time to draw them in.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.cond.threats: when threatening; targets <5 HD or outnumbered (Intimidation)
ACKS-HENCHMEN.cond.ownSettlement: with 0th-level locals of the home settlement
ACKS-HENCHMEN.cond.related: if related by blood or marriage
ACKS-HENCHMEN.cond.witnessed: when deeds are witnessed and recorded
ACKS-HENCHMEN.cond.sameReligion: hirelings of the same religion, when present
ACKS-HENCHMEN.mod.signingBonusHint: Paid on acceptance; Bribery proficiency cheapens the tiers
ACKS-HENCHMEN.mod.previousRefusalsHint: Cumulative −1 per prior attempt the candidate rejected (RR 162)
ACKS-HENCHMEN.mod.slanderHint: −1 per refuse-and-slander result against the party here (RR 162)
ACKS-HENCHMEN.mod.monsterMoraleHint: Independent monster: its own morale; defeated group: its previous leader's (MM 351)
ACKS-HENCHMEN.mod.effectiveLoyaltyHint: Base + permanent adjustments + employer CHA and powers
ACKS-HENCHMEN.mod.apparentLevelDiffHint: −1 per level the hireling believes he exceeds his employer (RR 168)
ACKS-HENCHMEN.mod.recentCasualties: Henchmen lost in the last month/adventure (−1)
ACKS-HENCHMEN.mod.riskShared: Similar risk to what others face
ACKS-HENCHMEN.outcomeHint.refuseSlander: All further reaction rolls by this party in this town/region suffer −1; this NPC can never be recruited by the party.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.outcomeHint.tryAgain: Reluctant — sweeten the deal (more pay, a magic item…) for another roll, else treat as Refuse.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.outcomeHint.acceptElan: Accepted in very good spirit: +1 to the hireling's loyalty score.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.outcomeHint.betrayal: Feigns acceptance to betray the adventurer when opportunity arises.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.outcomeHint.escape: Feigns acceptance to save its life; deserts at the first safe opportunity.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.outcomeHint.hesitate: Accepts with uncertain loyalties: starts at loyalty −2 instead of 0.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.outcomeHint.hostility: Leaves service immediately; considers the employer a rival and enemy; can never be re-hired.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.outcomeHint.resignation: Leaves service immediately, without ill will; could be recruited again later.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.outcomeHint.fanatic: A dedicated, sworn servant: loyalty score +1 (permanent).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.outcomeHint.refuses: Unwilling to follow orders. If the employer insists: −1 permanent loyalty, then roll again.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.outcomeHint.begrudging: Complies with minimum enthusiasm: −1 to related loyalty rolls or proficiency throws; quits early unless ordered on.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.throwUi.secretHint: The Judge rolls secretly: the result is whispered to the GM (RR 166).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.throwUi.gmOnlyOverride: Only the GM can unlock derived modifiers.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.location.name: Settlement name
ACKS-HENCHMEN.location.sourceDefault: Default (Class IV) — set urban families or an override
ACKS-HENCHMEN.location.alignmentHint: Directed searches for opposed-alignment classes shift one rarity step (chaotic warlocks hide in lawful towns). The default class ladders assume a lawful settlement — use a rarity-table variant to re-ladder other alignments.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.location.slanderHint: Each refuse-and-slander result gives the party −1 on further reaction rolls in this town/region (RR 162).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.location.marketLogHint: What the market did and when — the rollback record if the clock gets adjusted.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.location.processNowHint: Materialize due arrivals, charge due weekly fees, roll the month over if due.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.location.advanceWeekHint: Advances the world clock 7 days (all postings age).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.special.hint: The results of DIRECTED searches (specific class / proficiency, JJ 118) sit here until hired or the month re-rolls, alongside notable NPCs: drag an actor onto this sheet to list one (no time limit unless set via the hourglass). Hiring attempts are tracked per NPC everywhere.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.special.refusalsHint: Failed hiring attempts by this party against this NPC — each adds a cumulative −1 to further offers (RR 162).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.special.added: {name} is now available for hire here.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.special.empty: None. Drag an actor here, or found recruits will appear when registered.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.recruit.pickEmployer: Who is making the hiring offer?
ACKS-HENCHMEN.recruit.notAvailable: That candidate has not arrived yet (or is no longer available).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.recruit.slanderedBlock: That NPC refused and slandered the party — no further attempts are possible (RR 162).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.recruit.info: Offering employment to {name} (wage {wage} gp/month). Bribery proficiency: {bribery}.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.recruit.tryAgainChat: The candidate is reluctant. {employer} must sweeten the deal (better pay, a magic item…) to earn another roll — otherwise it counts as a refusal.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.hire.limitReached: {name} already leads {count} of {max} henchmen — hiring more will cost an existing henchman (RR 168).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.hire.hiredChat: {name} enters the service of {employer} at {wage} gp/month plus a half share of treasure.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.hire.error.limit: Henchman limit reached (blocked by world setting).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.gold.noCoins: {name} has no money items to credit.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.setting.wagesToBankHint: Wages credit each hireling's banked coin (quantitybank); disable to hand over carried coin instead.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.location.searchFeeHint: Rolled weekly per hireling type searched (RR 162), at this town's market class — the same rate for every recruiter.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.time.advanceDisabled: World-time advancement is disabled in the module settings.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.hire.queuedChat: {employer} sealed the deal — the recruit is reserved and their character sheet will be drawn up when a GM next connects.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.hire.error.actor-create-de: This seat cannot create the hireling's actor — the hire was queued for the next GM connection.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.card.calamityBody: {note} — a Hireling Loyalty roll is required (RR 166). The Judge rolls secretly and may adjust ±2.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.card.calamitySkip: Calamity recorded for {name} ({note}) — this hireling does not make loyalty rolls on calamities.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.card.waived: Loyalty roll waived by the Judge
ACKS-HENCHMEN.card.levelGainBody: {name} reached level {level} in service: loyalty +1 recorded. A Hireling Loyalty roll is due (RR 166).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.card.refusesBody: If the employer insists: −1 permanent loyalty, then roll Obedience again (RR 167).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.wage.nothingDue: No wages are due for {name}'s hirelings.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.wage.dueBody: {count} hireling(s) are owed a month's wages — {total} gp total. Missing wages is a calamity (RR 166).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.roster.title: Hirelings of {name}
ACKS-HENCHMEN.roster.limitHint: Maximum henchmen = 4 + CHA modifier + powers/proficiencies (RR 168). Mercenaries, specialists, and follower companions don't consume slots.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.roster.employerModsHint: Employer modifiers applied to every hireling's effective loyalty.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.roster.noSlot: Does not consume a henchman slot (follower apprentice/companion)
ACKS-HENCHMEN.roster.transfer: Transfer employment (recalculates loyalty; counts as calamity, RR 163)
ACKS-HENCHMEN.roster.empty: No hirelings yet. Recruit some at a Location's market.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.roster.calamityPlaceholder: e.g. missed rations, cursed, reduced to 0 hp
ACKS-HENCHMEN.roster.dismissBody: {name} leaves the roster. The actor is not deleted.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.monster.hdCap: A monster of {hd} HD cannot serve an employer of level {level} — its HD must be LOWER than the employer's level (MM 351).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.monster.animalGate: Recruiting normal animals requires Beast Friendship or Friends of Birds and Beasts (MM 351). Sapient beastmen/giants/humanoids/monstrosities/undead are open to anyone.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.monster.info: Recruiting {name} ({hd} HD — wage {wage} gp/month using HD as level, plus a half share if sapient).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.monster.feigned.betrayal: GM only: {name} only FEIGNS acceptance — it secretly loathes its employer and will betray the party when opportunity arises (Irrefusable Offer 2−).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.monster.feigned.escape: GM only: {name} only FEIGNS acceptance to save its life — it will desert at the first safe opportunity (Irrefusable Offer 3-5).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.followers.levelGate: {name} is level {level} — followers arrive at 9th level with a stronghold (RR 334).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.followers.title: Generate followers — {name}
ACKS-HENCHMEN.followers.strongholdMissin: No stronghold found (checked module APIs, flags, and inventory markers like "Stronghold: Castle"). The Judge decides whether followers arrive.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.followers.loyaltyFanatic: Fanatic followers: loyalty and morale start at +4 and they make NO loyalty rolls on calamities (morale rolls still apply).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.followers.loyaltyDefault: Followers start at loyalty +2, morale +1 over the equipment baseline.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.followers.slotNote: Apprentices and companions serve as henchmen WITHOUT consuming henchman slots; military followers act as mercenaries but follow on adventures (RR 335). Hire them with origin "follower" so the record marks them no-slot.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.followers.rolled: Follower grants rolled — see the GM chat card.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.command.noLocations: No recruitment locations exist yet. Create a Location actor first.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.setting.daysPerMonthHint: Used for wage cycles and monthly availability rolls (default 30).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.setting.advanceWorldTime: Allow module buttons to advance world time
ACKS-HENCHMEN.setting.advanceWorldTimeHi: Disable if another module (e.g. Simple Timekeeping) is the only clock authority.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.setting.autoRollCalamityHi: Skip the GM prompt and roll the secret loyalty check immediately when a calamity is detected.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.setting.enforceHenchmanLim: The 4 + CHA (+ powers) cap on henchmen (RR 168).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.setting.wageRemindersHint: Whisper the GM a wages-due card each game month per employer.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.setting.playerMarketVisibi: Which arrived candidates players can see on the recruitment board.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.setting.enableExpectedLivi: NPCs judge an adventurer's level by monthly spending (RR 173); enables apparent-level loyalty checks.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.setting.enableSlaveryHint: Optional custom rules; off by default. All slavery content stays hidden while disabled.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.slavery.cardNote: Optional RAW slavery rules (JJ 409). Availability follows the Hireling Availability tables; slaves displace part of the hireling pool. What is offered, and by whom, is the Judge's to reveal.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.slavery.lineProfessional: {label}: {mult}× free monthly wage − {less} gp ({upkeep}/month upkeep, base loyalty {loyalty})
ACKS-HENCHMEN.slavery.displacement: Where slavery is widespread, slaves replace {min}%–{max}% of the hirelings available.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.slavery.soldierUpkeep: Slave soldiers: {upkeep}/month upkeep (see Slave Troop Type costs by race).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.location.compositeHint: A settlement fields composite bowmen or longbowmen, never both (RR 164).
ACKS-HENCHMEN.candidate.searchHit: Found by your directed search — available all month.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.location.marketWeekHint: Week of the current market month. Arrivals land in weeks 1-3; late weeks are quiet by the book until the next monthly roll.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.posting.replaced: Search succeeded: {n} of the month's rolled henchmen now match what you sought (starred, available all month). Fee {fee} gp.
ACKS-HENCHMEN.posting.replacedNone: Search succeeded ({total} found) — but no rolled leveled henchmen remain in the market this month to re-sign. Fee {fee} gp; the post re-rolls next month.
